title: Gastrochilus denticulatus
taxonomy: Gastrochilus denticulatus is a species of orchid native to tropical Asia, and its classification is as follows: it belongs to the family Orchidaceae, subfamily Epidendroideae, tribe Vandeae, subtribe Aeridinae, and the genus Gastrochilus. The genus Gastrochilus is a group of small to medium-sized, terrestrial or epiphytic orchids commonly found in tropical forests. Gastrochilus denticulatus is further classified into the section Gastrochilus, which includes several species of orchids found in tropical Asia. The specific classification of Gastrochilus denticulatus helps in identifying and understanding its unique characteristics and distribution. This classification also aids in conservation efforts and research on this species of orchid.

intro: Gastrochilus denticulatus, also known as the Himalayan orchid, is a species of flowering plant in the family Orchidaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Gastrochilus denticulatus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
